---
title: "A frozen design, in plain sight"
subtitle: >-
  The full specification of the A=1 lattice has been printed on every page
  of this site since before the first paper — which is most of the answer
  to the retrodiction charge.
description: >-
  Twice the program has been accused of retrodiction — of tuning the
  lattice to reproduce known physics. But the design has no free
  parameters, its complete specification is the figure on every post, and
  that figure was committed weeks before the first paper and has never
  changed. Here is the distinction that charge misses.
author: "Jack D. Menendez"
date: "2026-06-09"
categories: [essay, methodology, retrodiction, falsifiability, audit]
---

Twice now the program has met the same objection: the wall of `PASS` rows
— the Dirac equation, the hydrogen spectrum to four significant figures,
gravitational time dilation — is **retrodiction**. The lattice, the charge
goes, was shaped to reproduce physics we already knew. It is the right
objection to raise, and it deserves a precise answer rather than a
defensive one.

The answer turns on a distinction the charge usually blurs.

## Two things "retrodiction" can mean

There is a **pejorative** sense and a **benign** sense, and they are not
the same accusation.

- *Pejorative:* you **tuned** the model to the answer. You had knobs, and
  you turned them until the output matched the data. This is overfitting,
  and it is fatal — a model with enough free parameters can fit anything
  and predicts nothing.
- *Benign:* you **reproduced** something already known. You derived the
  hydrogen spectrum, which was measured a century ago. This is not fatal
  at all; it is what every unification must do. General relativity had to
  reproduce Newton; quantum mechanics had to reproduce the spectral lines.

The charge that stings is the pejorative one. So answer it first — and
this program can answer it completely, because of how the design is built
and where it lives.

## There is nothing to tune

The A=1 lattice is a **minimal fixed object**: a bipartite octahedral
graph with six basis vectors, a single conservation axiom $\mathcal{A}=1$,
and one update rule (the bipartite tick). There are **no free parameters**
— the sole calibration is the lattice spacing that pins the grid to
physical units, and it is fixed by the Planck scale, not fitted to a
result. You cannot curve-fit a model that has nothing to fit. Whatever the
hydrogen Bohr radius comes out to ($k=0.0970$ against $0.0971$, with
nothing adjusted), it came out that way on its own. That is the difference
between a derivation and a fit, and it is structural, not rhetorical.

## The design is the wallpaper

Here is the part that makes the pejorative charge not just wrong but
*checkable* that it is wrong.

The complete specification of the lattice — all six basis vectors with
their coordinates, the conservation law, the chirality assignment, the
tick rule — is not buried in an appendix. **It is the figure on every page
of this site.** The social-card image that previews every post, every
essay, every paper landing page is the $T^3_\diamond$ legend: the nucleus
at $(0,0,0)$; $V_1=(1,1,1)$ RED, $V_2=(1,-1,-1)$ GREEN, $V_3=(-1,1,-1)$
BLUE on the RGB sublattice; their negatives as CMY; and
$\mathcal{A}=1 \Rightarrow |\psi_R|^2+|\psi_L|^2=1$ printed underneath. You
cannot read a single word of this program without seeing the entire
architecture it rests on. There is no hidden layer to adjust, because the
whole design is on the thumbnail.

And that figure is **older than the papers, and it has never changed.** Its
source — `figures/lattice.drawio` in the public Paper I repository — was
committed in **March 2026**, within days of the repository's first commit
and roughly seven weeks *before* Paper I's first Zenodo deposit. Its
earliest committed version already carried all six vectors in their final
form. You do not have to take my word for any of this: in the public repo,
`git log -S'(1,1,1)' -- figures/lattice.drawio` returns a **single
commit** — the value was written once and never touched. The same is true
of all six. The design was frozen before the results it is accused of
chasing, and the freeze is cryptographically dated in version control and
in the immutable, DOI-stamped Zenodo deposits.

So the pejorative retrodiction charge collapses on inspection. There were
no knobs; the design is public, complete, and visible on every page; and
it provably predates the physics it reproduces. "You retrofitted the
lattice to the answer" is refuted by the wallpaper.

## The honest other half

That clears the fatal charge. It does **not** clear the benign one, and I
will not pretend it does. Reproducing the hydrogen spectrum from a fixed
minimal axiom with no tuning is a *unification* — a real and difficult
achievement, the kind every foundational theory must earn — but it is not,
by itself, a *novel* prediction. A frozen design that recovers known
physics has shown it is **consistent** with the world, not yet that it
**outruns** the standard description.

The novelty lives elsewhere, and the program is explicit about where: in
the predictions that are not yet known to be true. The kinematic and
gauge-sector birefringence along the optical axis $(1,1,-1)$; the
multi-channel concordance along that axis; the quantum Roche limit and its
atomic-ISCO signatures. Those are the *bites* — and the
[claim map](../../../papers/claim-map.qmd) labels them honestly as `STUB`:
predicted with closed-form structure, not yet tested. That honesty is the
point. A program that reproduced everything and predicted nothing would be
the retrodiction the critics fear. This one reproduces a great deal from a
frozen, parameter-free, publicly-dated design — *and* stakes out novel,
falsifiable claims it could lose on.

## Geometry first, and it shows

In the spirit of the program's [Statement of Intent](../../../index.qmd):
none of this asserts the lattice *is* the substrate of reality. It asserts
something more modest and more checkable — that a single minimal geometric
object, fixed in advance and altered for no result, induces a startling
amount of known physics on its own. The `PASS` wall is what that object
does without being touched.

The design has been in plain sight the whole time. It is, quite literally,
the wallpaper.

## References

- [Paper I — *Geometry First*](../../../papers/paper-01-geometry-first.qmd)
  — the substrate, the six basis vectors, and the no-free-parameter
  experiment suite; figure source `figures/lattice.drawio`.
- [Claim map](../../../papers/claim-map.qmd) — the `PASS` wall, and the
  `STUB` novel predictions where the program stakes its falsifiable claims.
- [About the program](../../../about.qmd) — the audit system and what
  "proven" means here.
